title: CERIAS Binary Hardening Research
summary: Caterpillar-funded research through Purdue CERIAS on compiler-level binary hardening for ARM Cortex-M3 embedded systems running Zephyr RTOS.
description:
Caterpillar-funded research through Purdue CERIAS on compiler-level binary hardening for ARM Cortex-M3 embedded systems running Zephyr RTOS. Supervised with biweekly technical meetings with two Caterpillar representatives.

Confirmed GCC hardening flag availability on arm-zephyr-eabi-gcc SDK 0.17.0: stack protector family, FORTIFY_SOURCE, and fPIE are available; sanitizers and -fcf-protection are unavailable on this target.

Cross-referenced against Zephyr Kconfig: zero stack protector variants are exposed through Kconfig; FORTIFY_SOURCE is well-exposed; CONFIG_STACK_SENTINEL and CONFIG_MPU_STACK_GUARD are exposed but mutually exclusive.

Key finding: the FUNC_NO_STACK_PROTECTOR macro in gcc.h proves Zephyr was designed assuming global canary enablement — a significant architectural observation.

Targeting publication via cross-platform comparison with a FreeRTOS partner. Goal is a comprehensive survey of hardening flag availability and Kconfig exposure across both RTOS targets.

title: LLM Political Bias Research
summary: Mechanistic interpretability research with Purdue College of Liberal Arts using sparse autoencoders on Gemma-2-2B to identify political bias structures.
description:
Mechanistic interpretability research with Purdue College of Liberal Arts using sparse autoencoders on Gemma-2-2B.

Identified generalizable outgroup negativity node L18/15794 firing across controversial political, social, and religious prompts with activation scores ranging from 8.76 to 16.25. Secondary node L23/13919 co-activates in US-specific contexts only, revealing a two-tier architecture.

Steering experiments confirmed causality — the node is not merely correlated with the behavior but drives it.

Critical honest reframing: L18/15794 also fires on non-political prompts (cat preferences, classical music), making it a general outgroup negativity node rather than a politically specific one. This is an important nuance that prevents over-interpretation.

Next steps: completing negative controls, cross-model replication with Qwen prioritized for its non-Western training corpus, and potential Neuronpedia API automation for systematic feature mapping.

title: Data Mine Anomaly Detection
summary: ML anomaly detection research through the Purdue Data Mine learning community, applying machine learning methods for identifying anomalous patterns in datasets.
description:
Machine learning anomaly detection research project through the Purdue Data Mine learning community.

Applying machine learning methods for identifying anomalous patterns in datasets. The project focuses on building and evaluating detection models, understanding false positive rates, and developing practical approaches for real-world anomaly detection scenarios.

More details forthcoming as the project progresses.
